title: ART GC source code paths
key_conclusions: The real entry to ART GC source reading is `art/runtime/gc/heap.*`, not a single collector file.
The `collector/*` directory explains how a GC run executes, but root cause analysis still needs `root_visitor`, `reference_processor`, `space/*`, and runtime evidence.
A useful source-reading article must map source paths to logcat, meminfo, heap dump, and Perfetto signals.
shallow_points: This article is still an index-style walkthrough and does not include a function-level call chain screenshot from a concrete Android branch.
It does not yet verify the default CC or CMS combination, phase names, and log fields across Android versions.
It does not yet split `heap.cc` trigger logic into alloc GC, background GC, and explicit GC with a direct side-by-side table.
discovered_connections: Day 10 root and retained-path analysis now connects upstream to `root_visitor` in source.
Day 09 evidence-first reasoning now becomes a concrete source index: `heap -> collector -> reference/space -> logs/trace`.
Day 08 reference-type boundaries now connect directly to `reference_processor.*`.
suggest_future_topics: Day 12 should turn alloc GC, background GC, and explicit GC into a state diagram tied back to `heap.cc`.
A later article should decode common GC log fields such as freed bytes, paused, concurrent, and LOS against source phases.
The Profiler and MAT articles should reuse the Day 11 source-entry table as a source-to-tool-to-fix template.
knowledge_gaps: We still need to inspect the target Android branch and mark the exact call chain across `heap.cc`, `concurrent_copying.cc`, and `reference_processor.cc`.
We still need one real Perfetto example that aligns long pauses with collector phases and scheduler contention.
We still need to verify large-object-space and region-space interactions against concrete Android versions.
applied_reflections: Applied Day 10 next-run guidance by drawing the `heap -> collector -> root visitor -> reference processor` path explicitly.
Applied the long-term multi-diagram rule by replacing prose-heavy source commentary with diagrams, tables, and command blocks.
Applied the evidence-chain rule by adding `rg`, logcat, meminfo, heap dump, and Perfetto entry points.
visible_changes: Added three Mermaid diagrams because of the more-diagrams-less-prose feedback: source map, execution path, and troubleshooting flow.
Added source-entry, observation, boundary, and engineering-phrasing tables to reduce dense prose.
Added AOSP `rg` commands and a minimal observation command block to make the article more actionable.
next_run_instructions: Update README so Day 11 is marked complete and Day 12 remains pending.
Update `progress.json` so `current_day` becomes 12 and `last_generated` is refreshed.
Keep the Day 12 article visual-first and center it on alloc GC, background GC, and explicit GC trigger flow.
If GitHub Issues still cannot be read, keep recording the `gh auth login` or `GH_TOKEN` blocker in reflection, ledger, and automation memory.

issue_feedback.blocker: Could not read open Issues for hello-he/android-memory-expert because `gh issue list` requires `gh auth login` or `GH_TOKEN`.
